question:
A consumer survey investigating the ownership of television sets and cars for
a sample of 1000 families in a certain community finds the following results:
$$
\begin{array}{lll} 
& \text { TV } & \text { No TV } \\
\text { Car } & 115 & 245 \\
\text { No Car } & 380 & 260
\end{array}
$$
What is the probability that a family has
(i) a TV set?
(ii) a car?
(iii) both?
(iv) either a TV set or a car or both?

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
